mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >70% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that boosts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlled for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization that reduces input cost pressures and improves profit margins, predicting positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >65% of observations, or the signal reverses direction during inflationary supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains aggregate consumer spending power and corporate earnings stability, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with positive payroll growth show negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 240, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for earnings growth.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of contracting real GDP growth and rising federal funds rates creates a stagflationary pressure that simultaneously crushes earnings and raises discount rates, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The dual negative signal yields positive forward returns in >60% of quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate during a post-recession recovery phase signals improving labor market slack that supports consumer confidence and equity risk premiums, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Negative unemployment changes during recovery phases yield negative forward returns in >65% of cases, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward validation.
